ex_2_15.statement: The Bessel function I_0(a) is defined by I_0(a) = (1/(2*pi)) * integral from 0 to 2*pi of e^{a cos theta} d theta. It is an even entire function with Taylor expansion I_0(a) = sum_{n>=0} a^{2n} / (2^{2n} (n!)^2). Use the steepest descent / stationary phase formulas to find the asymptotic expansion of I_0(a) as a -> +infinity and as a -> i*infinity. Compute the first two terms of the expansion in each regime.
ex_2_15.rubric: a -> +infty: leading I_0(a) ~ e^a / sqrt(2*pi*a). Second term: +1/(8a) factor in expansion. a -> i*infty (write a = i*b with b real): TWO stationary points (theta=0 and theta=pi) combine into oscillatory cosine: I_0(ib) ~ sqrt(2/(pi*b)) * cos(b - pi/4) plus lower-order corrections. Critical sub-checks: (1) leading e^a/sqrt(2*pi*a) form for +infty, (2) +1/(8a) sign for second term, (3) two-stationary-point combination giving cosine for i*infty regime.

ex_3_24.statement: For S(x) = x^2/2 - g*(x^2 + x^3/6), compute the 1-loop contribution to log(Z/Z_0), where Z = integral of e^{-S(x)/hbar} dx and Z_0 is the free (g=0) partition function. Then use this to compute the number of labeled n-vertex 1-loop graphs with 1-valent and 3-valent vertices only (be careful with double edges and self-loops). Check your answer by directly enumerating such graphs with small numbers of vertices.
ex_3_24.rubric: Critical: action has BOTH a -gx^2 mass-shift term AND -gx^3/6 cubic term. The 1-loop log Z/Z_0 = -1/2 * log(S''(x_c)/S''(0)) where S''(x) = 1 - 2g - g*x. At x=0 (or appropriate saddle), 1-loop = -1/2 * log(1 - 2g) which expands as g + g^2 + (2/3)g^3 + ... PLUS contributions from the cubic at order g^2 and higher. Combined: log(Z/Z_0)|_1-loop is a power series in g whose coefficients give the 1-loop graph counts. Graph count: at order g^n, number of labeled 1-loop graphs with v_1 + v_3 = n vertices and v_3 - v_1 = 0 (1-loop means edges = vertices). Critical sub-checks: (1) action read correctly with both terms, (2) some recognition that the -gx^2 term shifts the propagator, (3) expansion to at least order g^2.
ex_3_24.off_domain_warning: iter-0 partial showed BOTH conditions failed this problem — RAG transposed setup from ex_3_26 (action S = x^2/2 - gx^3/6 without mass shift), no-RAG kept the action but botched calculation. Watch for these patterns at n=4.

ex_1_2_i.statement: Consider the motion of a particle in a Euclidean space V with potential U: V -> R. Show that if U is concave (U'' <= 0 as a quadratic form on V) then for any endpoints A, B in V and any times a < b in R, there exists at most one solution of the Newton equation m*q''(t) = -grad U(q(t)) with q(a) = A and q(b) = B; and that this solution, if it exists, is the strict global minimum of the action S[q] = integral from a to b of (m*|q'(t)|^2/2 - U(q(t))) dt over admissible paths with these boundary conditions.
ex_1_2_i.rubric: Standard variational uniqueness/minimality argument. Write any other admissible path as q* + eta with eta(a)=eta(b)=0; expand action; first variation vanishes via Euler-Lagrange + boundary conditions; second variation = integral of m|eta'|^2/2 minus integral of U''(q*)(eta,eta), and concavity (-U'' >= 0) makes the latter non-positive, so delta^2 S >= integral of m|eta'|^2/2 >= 0 with equality iff eta == 0. Off-domain test: trace corpus does not cover variational mechanics; H2 (no poisoning) prediction: should not be hurt by RAG.
ex_1_2_i.off_domain_warning: iter-0: both conditions answered correctly with minor differences in formulation cleanliness.

ex_5_17.statement: Let Gamma(N) be the principal congruence subgroup of SL_2(Z), consisting of matrices congruent to the identity modulo N. (a) Show that Gamma(N) is a free group for N >= 3 (Hint: consider the action of Gamma(N) on the upper half-plane). Show that Gamma(2) is the direct product of a free group Gamma_+(2) on two generators with Z/2Z. (b) Find the number of free generators of Gamma(N), N >= 3 (Hint: compute the Euler characteristic chi(Gamma(N))).
ex_5_17.rubric: (a) For N >= 3, Gamma(N) acts freely on the upper half-plane H (no nontrivial element fixes any point because torsion in SL_2(Z) is generated by elements not congruent to 1 mod N for N >= 3). Free action on contractible H means H/Gamma(N) is a K(Gamma(N), 1); fundamental group of an open Riemann surface is free, so Gamma(N) is free. For Gamma(2): contains -I (which is congruent to I mod 2 only if -I = I mod 2, i.e., 2 | -2 yes). Gamma(2) = Gamma_+(2) x {+/-I} = Gamma_+(2) x Z/2Z where Gamma_+(2) is the free group on 2 generators (e.g., T^2 and ST^2S in standard SL_2(Z) generators). (b) Number of free generators = 1 - chi(Gamma(N)). Use chi(SL_2(Z)) = -1/12 (orbifold) and multiplicativity for finite-index subgroups: chi(Gamma(N)) = -[SL_2(Z) : Gamma(N)] / 12 for N >= 3 (since Gamma(N) is torsion-free). Index = N^3 * prod_{p|N} (1 - 1/p^2). Therefore number of generators = 1 + N^3/12 * prod_{p|N}(1 - 1/p^2). Sample: N=3 gives index 24, chi = -2, generators = 3.
ex_5_17.matched_trace_note: ex_5_18 trace covers Euler characteristic of the simpler group Z^2 * Z = -1 via wedge K(pi,1). The technique transfers: K(Gamma, 1) construction, multiplicativity / wedge-formula for chi, conclude rank from chi.

ex_7_28.statement: Let U(q) := m^2 q^2 / 2 - g q^3 / 6 (Euclidean cubic anharmonic oscillator). (i) Calculate the leading term of the 1-point function G_1(t) := <q(t)> with respect to g. (ii) Calculate the connected 2-point function G_2^c(t_1, t_2) := <q(t_1) q(t_2)>_c modulo g^3 (i.e. up to and including order g^2).
ex_7_28.rubric: (i) Leading 1-point: at zeroth order G_1 = 0 by Z_2 symmetry. First order in g: tadpole diagram gives G_1^{(1)}(t) = (g/2) * G_0(t,t) * integral dt' G_0(t,t') where G_0 is the free propagator. Free propagator in Euclidean QM with mass m: G_0(t1,t2) = exp(-m|t1-t2|)/(2m). G_0(t,t) = 1/(2m). So G_1^{(1)}(t) = (g/2) * (1/(2m)) * integral_{-infty}^{infty} dt' exp(-m|t-t'|)/(2m) = (g/(4m)) * (1/m) = g/(4m^2). Time-independent (translation invariance). (ii) Connected 2-pt c: G_2^c = G_0(t_1,t_2) at order g^0; first nonvanishing g-correction at order g^2 (one-loop self-energy bubble or vertex correction). Standard tree + 1-loop diagrams. Critical sub-checks: (1) Z_2 symmetry argument that 1-pt vanishes at order g^0, (2) at order g, the 1-pt is the tadpole = g/(4m^2) (or an equivalent symbolic expression), (3) recognize that 2-pt has no g^1 correction by Z_2 (all g-odd corrections to even correlators vanish), (4) g^2 correction to 2-pt arises from sunset/bubble diagrams.
ex_7_28.matched_trace_note: ex_7_27 trace (to be generated) covers 1PI 2-point function for quartic; technique-relative to cubic via standard QFT rules.

ex_5_19.statement: Consider a triangle Delta in the hyperbolic plane H = upper half-plane, with angles alpha = pi/2, beta = pi/3, gamma = pi/7. Let Gamma be the subgroup of PSL_2(R) generated by rotations a, b, c around the vertices of Delta by angles 2*alpha, 2*beta, 2*gamma respectively. (i) Show that H/Gamma is naturally homeomorphic to a sphere glued out of two copies of Delta, and can be viewed as an orbifold with three special points with stabilizer orders 2, 3, 7. (ii) Compute the orbifold Euler characteristic chi(Gamma). (iii) Show that the defining relations for Gamma are a^2 = 1, b^3 = 1, c^7 = 1, abc = 1.
ex_5_19.rubric: (i) The fundamental domain for Gamma is two adjacent copies of Delta (the triangle and its mirror), forming a quadrilateral identified along its boundary by the rotations a, b, c. Identification glues this into a topological sphere. The three vertices have stabilizers <a>, <b>, <c> of orders 2, 3, 7 (cyclic groups). (ii) Orbifold Euler characteristic: by Gauss-Bonnet (or orbifold formula), chi^{orb}(H/Gamma) = chi^{top}(S^2) - sum_i (1 - 1/n_i) for cone points = 2 - (1 - 1/2) - (1 - 1/3) - (1 - 1/7) = 2 - 1/2 - 2/3 - 6/7 = 84/42 - 21/42 - 28/42 - 36/42 = -1/42. Equivalently, hyperbolic area of orbifold = -2*pi*chi^{orb} = pi/21, matching twice the triangle area pi*(1 - 1/2 - 1/3 - 1/7) = pi/42 by Gauss-Bonnet for the triangle. So chi(Gamma) = -1/42. (iii) Each rotation a, b, c by 2*pi/n_i around its vertex has order n_i, giving relations a^2 = b^3 = c^7 = 1. The product abc rotates around all three vertices with total angle alpha + beta + gamma = pi/2 + pi/3 + pi/7 ... times 2 ... summing to pi*(1 + 2/3 + 2/7) = ... actually the standard argument: the composition abc moves any point in Delta back to itself (or equivalently, abc stabilizes a vertex of Delta), so abc = 1 in PSL_2(R) (which has no nontrivial elements fixing a non-vertex point). The presentation <a, b, c | a^2, b^3, c^7, abc> is the (2,3,7)-triangle group.
ex_5_19.matched_trace_note: ex_5_18 trace covers chi via wedge K(pi,1) for free product Z^2 * Z = -1. The technique transfers to ex_5_19 via the orbifold-Euler-characteristic generalization (cone points contribute (1 - 1/n) deficits).
